title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple
review: A really fun original story told in the 28-verse, without many zombies, or the usual plot beats you'd expect from zombies. There's some deeply British lore to wrap your head around that might be lost on outsiders, and also the first and last scenes from {28 Years Later} are required watching in order for any of this to make sense. I really liked it, and especially enjoyed Ralph Fiennes' performance and his electrifying scene near the end. More and more, the generic nature of {28 Weeks Later} stands out compared to imaginative stories told in the rest of the franchise. I hope they continue to receive funding for more instalments.
